mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate trend lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires, measured across all available trigger occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in housing starts and elevated federal funds rate jointly signal restrictive monetary conditions transmitting into interest-rate-sensitive construction demand, amplifying negative wealth-effect feedback into equities beyond what either series predicts alone, producing negative 20-day SPX returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline > 5% AND FEDFUNDS above 3-year moving average
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing stress
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient (HOUST decline x FEDFUNDS level) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression predicting 20-day forward returns, or single-series HOUST model performs equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion regime reflects sustained corporate output and capacity utilization, supporting near-term earnings momentum and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO monthly growth sign and 5-day forward SPX return direction is not significantly different from zero (Fisher exact test p > 0.10) in late-cycle subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and retail sales growth signals a synchronized weakening of labor income and consumer spending that jointly forecasts a demand contraction not captured by either series individually, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1%
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are jointly met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of occurrences, or interaction model does not outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation crosses above a threshold consistent with restrictive policy expectations, markets price in higher future rate hikes, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following CPI threshold breach exceed the unconditional historical average return in more than 50% of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and forecasts continued near-term corporate revenue strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week irrespective of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS monthly growth and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 across the full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where real GDP growth decelerates below its trailing 4-quarter average, equity markets anticipate an approaching earnings slowdown and price in a de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this effect is absent or reversed during early-cycle recoveries when deceleration reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration below trailing 4-quarter average is not associated with negative 60-day forward SPX returns (mean return not significantly below zero, t-test p > 0.10) when restricted to late-cycle expansion periods.
